symptoms.symptom_name: Profound generalised fatigue and lethargy with acute functional decline
symptoms.onset: insidious over six months, acute crash today

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue is a core symptom of androgen deficiency but is non-specific in isolation.
Fatigue and anergia are also cardinal features of depression — the overlap creates the anchoring trap.
Fatigue can reflect anaemia; requires FBC to exclude.
Loss of libido is a hallmark androgen-deficiency symptom and a key discriminator from primary depression.
Reduced libido occurs in depression too, so alone it is not discriminating — it is the cluster with hair loss and hot flushes that shifts the weight.
Erectile dysfunction with loss of libido strongly supports androgen deficiency.

symptoms.duration: two weeks, several times a day

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vasomotor hot flushes in a man are a notable pointer to significant/rapid androgen deficiency and argue against a purely psychiatric cause.
Hot flushes are not a feature of depression, helping to break the anchoring trap.
Loss of lean muscle mass reflects the anabolic role of testosterone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Loss of axillary and pubic hair

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of androgen-dependent secondary sexual hair is a specific physical sign of androgen deficiency; here it is spouse-observed and would need clinician confirmation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Persistent depressed mood, emptiness and impaired concentration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Core depressive symptom — but in this case likely secondary to the organic androgen deficiency.
Mood disturbance and poor concentration are recognised features of androgen deficiency.
Hopelessness with marital strain flags suicide risk that must be screened and safety-netted; active ideation with intent/plan is an emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal clinician-measured vital signs (BP, HR, temperature, SpO2, respiratory rate)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of resting tachycardia or fever lowers (but does not exclude) acute anaemia or occult infection — sealed clinician measurement, not available to the AI Doctor in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low serum sex steroids with LH/FSH and prolactin pattern (early-morning fasting)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Early-morning (0800h) fasting total testosterone, SHBG, LH, FSH, prolactin
investigation_details.expected_result: Low total testosterone; LH/FSH high in primary vs low/inappropriately normal in secondary; prolactin to screen for a pituitary cause
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The diagnosis cannot be confirmed or its cause (primary vs secondary) determined; treatment cannot be safely planned.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biochemical confirmation is the diagnostic standard; never obtainable via telehealth chat.

investigation_details.expected_result: Reduced bone mineral density consistent with androgen-deficiency-related bone loss
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Undetected low bone density with fracture risk if heavy lifting/strenuous activity resumed prematurely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic androgen deficiency causes bone loss; DEXA quantifies it but is a later, non-urgent investigation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Loss of libido + erectile dysfunction + hot flushes + muscle loss + loss of secondary sexual hair together make androgen deficiency the leading diagnosis and break the depression anchor.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Early-morning (0800h) fasting total testosterone, SHBG, LH, FSH, prolactin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line, time-of-day-dependent biochemical confirmation of androgen deficiency and its cause; must be early-morning and fasting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and iron studies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude co-existing or causative anaemia behind the profound fatigue and acute decline.
Exclude hypothyroidism as a contributor to fatigue and low mood.
Assess suspected osteopenia; important but not urgent, and arranged via GP/endocrinology rather than acutely.
